title: First consented batch: at least 50 places, at least 2 locales, 100% consent+provenance
context: M1 proves the pipeline on a real, fully-consented batch of place-name recordings. Every entry must pass the CI consent-completeness, license-allowlist, and provenance gates. No Indigenous/minority-language names may be included unless a community-steward record exists. IPA (narrow and/or broad) and an English respelling must be provided on at least 90% of entries. Each Recording must carry a BCP-47 language tag, accent/dialect label, and a variantType label (local|official|anglicised|historical|other). The batch must span at least 2 locales/language communities. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-pipeline-001 and place-name-pronunciation-license-002.
objective: Collect, process, and commit at least 50 place-name entries across at least 2 locales, each with at least 1 consented and provenanced recording, IPA+respelling on at least 90% of entries, variantType labels, and 100% CI-passing consent+provenance records. No Indigenous/minority names without a community-steward record.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 50 distinct place entries with at least 1 Recording each are committed and pass all CI gates.
At least 2 distinct locales (language/region combinations identified by BCP-47 tags) are represented.
100% of Recordings carry a complete, valid ConsentRecord and a resolvable provenance link; CI gates pass.
IPA (narrow and/or broad) plus English respelling are present on at least 90% of place entries in the batch.
Every Recording carries a BCP-47 language tag, an accent/dialect label, and a variantType label.
No Indigenous or minority-language place name is included unless a community-steward record is in place.
Privacy/consent reviewer and cultural reviewer both sign off the batch before the task is marked done.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass on the committed batch.
output: Corpus data directory with at least 50 place-name entries (JSON records + audio artifacts) committed via PR, passing all CI gates, with dual reviewer sign-off.
